quick_wins.title: Electricity Regulation Amendment Act — secondary legislation
quick_wins.description: Complete regulations establishing NERSA's operational independence and the governance framework for the competitive electricity market. ERA Amendment Act signed August 2024; implementing regulations required within 6 months.

quick_wins.title: IRP 2024 gazette and approval
quick_wins.description: Finalise and gazette the Integrated Resource Plan 2024, establishing the electricity capacity mix to 2030. Replaces the stalled IRP 2019 update and provides investor certainty for REIPPP procurement rounds.

quick_wins.title: REIPPP Round 7 preferred bidder announcement
quick_wins.description: Close Round 7 bid window and announce preferred bidders for 2,500MW of new renewable capacity. Operation Vulindlela priority deliverable for H1 2025.

quick_wins.title: Durban port concession framework — Phase 1
quick_wins.description: Publish TNPA guidelines for private terminal operator concessions at Durban Container Terminal. First step in Transnet Port Terminals reform under Operation Vulindlela. Critical for reducing port dwell time from current 8 days.

quick_wins.title: Single Window customs system rollout
quick_wins.description: Deploy SARS/ITAC integrated Single Window portal at all major ports of entry, eliminating parallel documentation processes. Operation Vulindlela customs reform milestone.

quick_wins.title: ICASA spectrum licensing — 3.5GHz band
quick_wins.description: Complete assignment of high-demand spectrum licences (2.6GHz and 3.5GHz) to mobile network operators following the 2023 auction. Enables 5G rollout and broadband densification.

medium_term.title: National Transmission Company formation (NTC-SA)
medium_term.description: Complete the legal and operational unbundling of Eskom's transmission function into an independent National Transmission Company. Central to Operation Vulindlela's energy reform programme — enables open-access grid and third-party wheeling.

medium_term.title: Competitive electricity market — Day-Ahead Market
medium_term.description: Implement NERSA-regulated Day-Ahead Market allowing multiple generators to compete for dispatch. Requires NTC operational independence and metering infrastructure. Key to attracting private investment in generation.

medium_term.title: Durban Container Terminal concession — Phase 2 award
medium_term.description: Complete competitive tender and award concession for DCT Pier 1 or Pier 2 to a private terminal operator. Target: reduce dwell time from 8 days to under 4 days within 18 months of concession start.

medium_term.title: Open-access freight rail — Network Statement implementation
medium_term.description: Publish and implement a Network Statement allowing third-party operators access to the Transnet freight rail network on commercial terms. Prerequisite for private rail investment on the iron ore and manganese corridors.

medium_term.title: National Water Resource Infrastructure Agency operationalisation
medium_term.description: Establish and staff the NWRIA as a dedicated infrastructure delivery vehicle for bulk water schemes. Separates water resource infrastructure from DWS regulatory and operational functions.
medium_term.legislative_reference: Water and Sanitation Laws Amendment Act 2023

medium_term.title: Transmission grid expansion — 14,000km new lines
medium_term.description: Approve and begin procurement for the transmission grid expansion plan required to integrate planned REIPPP capacity. Current grid constraints are the binding limit on renewable energy integration.

medium_term.title: SA Connect Phase 2 — broadband rollout
medium_term.description: Implement universal service broadband rollout using USAF funding and DCDT oversight. Target: 80% household internet access by 2027. Includes school and clinic connectivity as priority.

long_term.title: Full competitive retail electricity market
long_term.description: Enable competition at the retail level, allowing large industrial consumers and eventually households to choose their electricity supplier. Final stage of the Electricity Regulation Amendment Act implementation pathway.

long_term.title: Durban port dwell time — 8 days to 3 days
long_term.description: Achieve world-class port productivity through private terminal concessions, digitalisation, and infrastructure investment. Full transformation requires 3–5 years of concession operation and capital expenditure.

long_term.title: 50% non-Eskom power generation by 2030
long_term.description: Diversify South Africa's generation mix to 50% non-Eskom (private IPPs, municipalities, C&I) through REIPPP, self-generation, and embedded generation programmes. Eliminates structural load-shedding risk.

long_term.title: Municipal water network rehabilitation
long_term.description: Reduce non-revenue water (current national average ~45%) by rehabilitating ageing municipal pipe networks. Requires MISA technical support, conditional grants, and DWS enforcement of Blue Drop standards.

long_term.title: Durban–Gauteng freight corridor integration
long_term.description: Achieve seamless road-rail-port logistics for the busiest freight corridor in sub-Saharan Africa, reducing logistics costs by 20–25%. Requires integrated corridor governance and private rail operator entry.

key_dependencies.description: Without an independent regulator, market rules lack credibility and private investors cannot price risk. NERSA independence is the legal foundation for all market reform.
Grid expansion requires capital spending Eskom cannot fund without balance sheet repair. Debt restructuring and R254bn government support package must be completed first.
Open-access wheeling and competitive dispatch require a neutral transmission company separated from Eskom generation interests.
Private terminal operators bring capital, technology, and productivity incentives that state-owned TNPA cannot replicate. Port reform is the precondition for export competitiveness.

risk_factors.title: Eskom union resistance to unbundling
risk_factors.description: NUM and solidarity have historically opposed Eskom restructuring. NTC-SA formation could face industrial action or legal challenge, delaying grid independence.

risk_factors.title: Grid congestion limiting RE integration
risk_factors.description: Transmission grid capacity is the binding constraint on REIPPP generation — awarded projects cannot connect. If the 14,000km grid expansion programme is delayed, new generation becomes stranded.

risk_factors.title: REIPPP financing costs and currency risk
risk_factors.description: IPP project finance is predominantly USD-denominated while revenue is ZAR. Rand depreciation raises tariffs and reduces investor returns, potentially stalling bid windows.

risk_factors.title: Transnet governance and balance sheet fragility
risk_factors.description: Transnet's R130bn debt and operational mismanagement constrain its capacity to invest in port and rail infrastructure even under reform. Concession processes require functional governance.

risk_factors.title: Regulatory capture of NERSA
risk_factors.description: NERSA's history of politically influenced tariff decisions and capacity constraints could undermine market rule-setting even after formal independence.

quick_wins.title: SMME Ombud Service operationalisation
quick_wins.description: Activate the Small Enterprise Ombud Service under the National Small Enterprise Amendment Act 2023. The Ombud resolves B2B payment disputes between SMMEs and large firms without court action — a critical cash flow protection mechanism.

quick_wins.title: BizPortal 2.0 — single registration integration
quick_wins.description: Integrate CIPC, SARS tax registration, UIF, Workmen's Compensation, and business bank account opening into a single digital registration journey. Reduce business registration from 5 days and 6 agencies to 1 day.

quick_wins.title: 30-day payment rule enforcement directive
quick_wins.description: Issue a binding directive under the PFMA requiring all national departments and public entities to pay SMME invoices within 30 days. Direct instruction from the Presidency under Operation Vulindlela.

quick_wins.title: Home-based business zoning — model municipal by-law
quick_wins.description: Publish a model municipal by-law permitting home offices, tutoring, repair workshops, and small-scale food production in residential zones. Reduces regulatory barrier for the most accessible entry point to self-employment.

quick_wins.title: BBBEE compliance cost reduction for micro-enterprises
quick_wins.description: Issue regulations exempting enterprises below R10m turnover from BBBEE verification fees and simplifying annual compliance reporting. Reduces regulatory burden on the smallest firms.

medium_term.title: SEFA mandate expansion — R8bn annual lending envelope
medium_term.description: Increase SEFA's capitalisation and annual lending envelope from ~R2.5bn to R8bn. Requires National Treasury allocation and a revised SEFA mandate prioritising direct lending over wholesale (blended finance vehicles).

medium_term.title: SMME set-aside: 30% of government procurement
medium_term.description: Mandate that 30% of all government tenders above R500,000 be awarded to qualifying SMMEs and cooperatives. Implement monitoring through NT procurement portal and publish quarterly compliance reports.

medium_term.title: Informal economy integration — simplified tax and trading infrastructure
medium_term.description: Introduce a simplified presumptive tax for informal traders below R500k turnover, and develop dignified urban trading infrastructure (covered markets, ablution facilities, storage) in at least 20 municipalities.

medium_term.title: Township economic zones — fast-track approvals
medium_term.description: Designate 50 township enterprise zones with simplified zoning, infrastructure grants, and reduced municipal levies. Builds on Gauteng and Western Cape pilot programmes.

medium_term.title: CCMA dispute resolution — 30-day target
medium_term.description: Increase CCMA commissioner capacity and implement a 30-day maximum for conciliation and arbitration of unfair dismissal and labour disputes. Reduces employment uncertainty for small firms.

medium_term.title: Replacement angel investor tax incentive (post-Section 12J)
medium_term.description: Design and implement a replacement for the Section 12J venture capital incentive (expired 2021) focused on early-stage SMME equity. Target R5bn in private co-investment over 3 years.

long_term.title: Alternative credit scoring for SMMEs
long_term.description: Enable credit bureaux and banks to use alternative data (utility payments, mobile money, SARS tax history) for SMME credit assessment. Unlock formal finance for the estimated 700,000 creditworthy but unscored small firms.

long_term.title: National Small Business Development Bank
long_term.description: Establish a dedicated development finance institution for SMMEs, consolidating SEFA, NEF small-business functions, and SEDA financial services. Modelled on Germany's KfW or Brazil's BNDES SME division.

long_term.title: Informal sector formalization — 500,000 new registrations
long_term.description: Achieve 500,000 new formal business registrations from the informal sector by 2030 through combined incentives: tax simplification, social protection eligibility, access to public procurement.

key_dependencies.description: Single-window registration is the prerequisite for all downstream SMME interventions — firms must exist formally before they can access SEFA, procurement set-asides, or Ombud protection.
Government late payment is the most commonly cited cash flow killer for small suppliers. Rule enforcement is a no-cost intervention with immediate impact on SMME viability.
Without adequate capitalisation, SEFA cannot serve as a meaningful alternative to commercial banks for SMME lending. NT budget allocation is the binding constraint.

risk_factors.title: Municipal resistance to by-law reform
risk_factors.description: Municipalities jealously guard zoning and trading by-laws as revenue sources. Home-based business and informal trading reforms face pushback from local government officials and established business associations.

risk_factors.title: SEFA capitalisation constraints
risk_factors.description: National Treasury's fiscal consolidation programme limits new allocations to SEFA. Without additional capital, the lending envelope expansion cannot materialise.

risk_factors.title: Large firm procurement dominance
risk_factors.description: Existing procurement relationships and compliance complexity favour large incumbents even under set-aside rules. Enforcement of the 30% SMME set-aside requires active monitoring and consequences for non-compliance.

risk_factors.title: Labour law deterrents to formal hiring
risk_factors.description: SMME owners consistently cite Labour Relations Act and BCEA dismissal procedures as a reason to avoid formal employment. Without LRA amendment, formal job creation remains constrained.

risk_factors.title: Political economy of informal sector regulation
risk_factors.description: Informal traders and taxi operators represent organized constituencies. Formalisation and trading infrastructure reforms can trigger political backlash if poorly sequenced.

quick_wins.title: National Reading Programme — 1,000 priority schools
quick_wins.description: Launch targeted structured literacy deployment in the 1,000 lowest-performing primary schools (Quintile 1–2). Based on DBE's Early Grade Reading Study, phonics-based structured literacy can raise Grade 3 reading scores by 0.3–0.5 SD in 18 months.

quick_wins.title: CAPS-aligned structured literacy curriculum — Grades 1–3
quick_wins.description: Mandate phonics sequencing and decodable texts in the CAPS curriculum for Home Language in Grades 1–3. Requires teacher guides, workbooks, and assessor training — all within the current DBE expenditure envelope.
quick_wins.legislative_reference: SA Schools Act / CAPS Curriculum Framework

quick_wins.title: TVET accreditation backlog — QCTO clearance
quick_wins.description: Clear the QCTO backlog of TVET college programme accreditations, qualification part-qualifications, and assessment centre approvals. Estimated 2,000+ applications stalled. Blocking artisan certification.

quick_wins.title: ECD practitioner registration framework
quick_wins.description: Publish the qualifications pathway and registration framework for ECD practitioners under the Social Service Professions Act. Required to professionalise the 100,000+ person ECD workforce and unlock subsidy eligibility.

quick_wins.title: Digital skills roadmap — TVET and youth
quick_wins.description: Publish a National Digital Skills Framework mapping ICT qualifications across TVET, community colleges, and higher education to labour market demand. Coordinates SETAs, MICT SETA, and DHET programming.

medium_term.title: Reading coaches deployed to all primary schools
medium_term.description: Scale the structured literacy programme from 1,000 pilot schools to all 25,000+ primary schools through a national reading coach deployment programme. Estimated 5,000 coaches at R400m annual cost.

medium_term.title: TVET artisan pipeline — energy transition trades
medium_term.description: Increase artisan apprenticeship targets specifically for electricians, solar PV technicians, wind turbine technicians, and green hydrogen plant operators. Align MERSETA and EWSETA funding with REIPPP sector needs.

medium_term.title: NHI Phase 1 — district health management and purchasing
medium_term.description: Establish District Health Management Offices and pilot the purchasing function under the NHI Act in 3 pilot districts. Tests the contracting model before national rollout. NHI Act signed May 2024.

medium_term.title: NSFAS sustainability reform
medium_term.description: Implement means-testing improvements, bursary cap administration, and direct payments to institutions to stabilise NSFAS after the administrative collapse of 2023–2024. Critical to TVET and university throughput.

medium_term.title: Universal Grade R access by 2026
medium_term.description: Extend Grade R (preschool year) to all learners through a combination of public schools, ECD centres, and subsidy expansion. Foundation for reading readiness intervention.

medium_term.title: Structured teacher development programme
medium_term.description: Implement a national teacher coaching and professional development programme focused on literacy instruction. Built on lesson observation and feedback — distinct from traditional workshop-based CPD.

long_term.title: PIRLS Grade 4 reading: 19% to 40% proficiency by 2030
long_term.description: Achieve a doubling of Grade 4 reading proficiency in the PIRLS assessment from the 2021 baseline of 19% (worst among middle-income countries). Requires sustained structured literacy and teacher development from 2025.

long_term.title: TVET artisan certifications: 20,000 to 50,000 per year
long_term.description: Triple annual artisan certification output by transforming TVET colleges from compliance-driven to industry-integrated institutions. Requires SETA reform, industry partnerships, and facility investment.

long_term.title: Universal health coverage — NHI full rollout
long_term.description: Achieve broad universal health coverage for primary and secondary care through the NHI Fund by 2030. Requires successful Phase 1 pilots, private sector contracting model, and sustained fiscal commitment.

long_term.title: 1 million youth with ICT certifications
long_term.description: Credential 1 million young South Africans with marketable ICT qualifications by 2030 through TVET, community colleges, and digital skills partnerships with industry (Microsoft, Google, AWS cloud programmes).

key_dependencies.description: Children who cannot read for meaning by Grade 4 rarely recover academically. Foundation literacy is the single highest-leverage intervention in the human capital pipeline.
ECD quality determines the cognitive and language baseline children bring to Grade 1. Without ECD investment, the reading programme starts from a lower baseline.
Industrial strategy and green transition reforms (Package 4) depend on a supply of qualified artisans. TVET transformation is the upstream requirement.
NSFAS collapse in 2023–2024 disrupted hundreds of thousands of students. Sustainable NSFAS funding is required before throughput targets are meaningful.

risk_factors.title: Teacher union resistance to curriculum reform
risk_factors.description: SADTU has historically opposed structured literacy mandates as undermining teacher professional autonomy. Implementation requires DBE to navigate union relations carefully while maintaining curriculum accountability.

risk_factors.title: NSFAS funding collapse
risk_factors.description: Recurring administrative failures and underfunding of NSFAS create student and institution instability. A second major NSFAS crisis would undermine both TVET and university throughput targets.

risk_factors.title: ECD practitioner pay and conditions
risk_factors.description: ECD practitioners earn below minimum wage in most non-profit centres. Without pay equity with Grade R teachers, the ECD workforce cannot be professionalised or retained.

risk_factors.title: NHI constitutional and legal challenges
risk_factors.description: Medical associations, private hospital groups, and some provinces are litigating the NHI Act. Court decisions could delay or fundamentally alter implementation.

risk_factors.title: Education procurement corruption
risk_factors.description: Structural corruption in textbook, stationery, and school infrastructure procurement in some provinces has historically wasted education resources and delayed curriculum implementation.

quick_wins.title: Critical Minerals Beneficiation Strategy — gazette
quick_wins.description: Publish the DMRE Critical Minerals Strategy as a gazetted government policy, establishing SA's position on PGMs, manganese, vanadium, and lithium beneficiation targets. Required to unlock EU critical minerals partnership investment.

quick_wins.title: EV White Paper — Cabinet approval
quick_wins.description: Secure Cabinet approval for the Electric Vehicles White Paper setting out SA's managed automotive transition strategy. Resolves the policy uncertainty that is delaying OEM investment decisions on EV assembly in SA.

quick_wins.title: AfCFTA National Action Plan 2024–2026
quick_wins.description: Gazette South Africa's AfCFTA implementation priorities — tariff schedules, sanitary and phytosanitary measures, and services sector commitments — providing business with the regulatory roadmap for continental expansion.

quick_wins.title: AGOA utilisation improvement plan
quick_wins.description: Publish a sector-by-sector AGOA utilisation strategy targeting undercaptured categories (apparel, processed food, automotive parts). SA currently uses AGOA preferences for <40% of eligible exports.

quick_wins.title: SEZ approvals — reduce to 6 months
quick_wins.description: Reduce the SEZ establishment and licence approval period from the current 18+ months to 6 months through process reform and a single window for SEZ investors. Immediate action available within existing DTIC mandate.

medium_term.title: AfCFTA tariff liberalisation — 90% of goods
medium_term.description: Implement 90% of SA's goods tariff reduction commitments under AfCFTA, phased over 2025–2028. Requires ITAC schedule amendments and customs system updates. Unlocks continental market access for manufacturers.

medium_term.title: Green Hydrogen IPP Programme — 1GW electrolyzer
medium_term.description: Launch a dedicated green hydrogen IPP procurement round under the REIPPP framework, targeting 1GW of electrolyzer capacity. Requires off-take agreement framework and port hydrogen bunkering infrastructure.

medium_term.title: APDP Phase 2 — EV transition alignment
medium_term.description: Revise the Automotive Production and Development Programme incentives to reward investment in EV component manufacturing (battery packs, electric drivetrains) alongside ICE production. Prevents SA from being stranded in the EV transition.

medium_term.title: Battery minerals processing — 3 new facilities
medium_term.description: Secure investment for three new battery minerals beneficiation facilities: PGM refining expansion, lithium cathode materials, and vanadium flow battery production. Requires DFI co-investment and streamlined environmental authorisations.

medium_term.title: SA–EU Critical Minerals Partnership
medium_term.description: Finalise and operationalise the SA-EU Strategic Minerals Partnership announced in 2023, securing European investment commitments for beneficiation in exchange for preferential minerals supply access.

medium_term.title: Competition Commission digital markets inquiry — implementation
medium_term.description: Implement the findings of the ongoing digital markets inquiry into platform dominance, data portability, and interoperability. Enables SA fintech and digital economy firms to compete against global platforms.

long_term.title: Green hydrogen exports — 500,000 tonnes by 2030
long_term.description: Establish South Africa as a competitive green hydrogen and green ammonia exporter, leveraging its renewable energy resources and PGM-based catalyst production. Requires port infrastructure, pipeline investment, and off-take agreements with European utilities.

long_term.title: Battery minerals beneficiation — R50bn investment
long_term.description: Attract R50bn in new investment in battery minerals beneficiation by 2030, moving SA up the value chain from raw ore to cathode materials and battery components. Requires long-term mining rights stability and competitive electricity prices.

long_term.title: AfCFTA — R100bn additional continental exports
long_term.description: Achieve R100bn in additional exports to continental Africa by 2030 through AfCFTA tariff liberalisation, pan-African payment systems, and customs corridor agreements.

long_term.title: EV manufacturing — 10% of domestic vehicle output
long_term.description: Achieve 10% of South African vehicle production being EV by 2030, through APDP incentive alignment, OEM investment attraction, and local EV component supply chain development.

key_dependencies.description: Green hydrogen competitiveness depends entirely on the price of renewable electricity. SA's green hydrogen exports are only viable if REIPPP delivers electricity below R0.50/kWh — requiring Package 1 success.
SEZs and APDP beneficiation depend on a supply of qualified manufacturing workers. TVET artisan pipeline transformation is the upstream prerequisite.
AfCFTA and AGOA export competitiveness improvements are negated if port logistics costs add 15–30% to export costs. Package 1 port reform is a precondition for competitive exports.
SEZ investment attraction requires credible governance, reliable customs, and IP protection. Without Package 5 state capacity reform, investor confidence in SEZ commitments remains weak.

success_metrics.metric: Green hydrogen first commercial export contract

risk_factors.title: AGOA renewal uncertainty
risk_factors.description: AGOA expires in September 2025. US Congressional renewal is uncertain and SA's geopolitical positioning (BRICS, Russia relations) creates political risk. Non-renewal would cost R50bn+ in automotive and textile exports.

risk_factors.title: EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)
risk_factors.description: EU CBAM full implementation from 2026 will impose carbon costs on SA steel, aluminium, and cement exports. SA's carbon pricing system is not yet accepted as equivalent — exports face a 20–30% cost increase.

risk_factors.title: Chinese EV competition undermining APDP
risk_factors.description: BYD, Chery, and SAIC are entering the SA domestic market at prices that undercut APDP-supported local assembly. If OEMs lose domestic market share, the volume basis for APDP export incentives collapses.

risk_factors.title: Water and land constraints on green hydrogen
risk_factors.description: Industrial-scale green hydrogen requires large quantities of desalinated water and land for solar/wind farms. Environmental authorisation backlogs and water licence delays could stall projects.

risk_factors.title: Power cost competitiveness for beneficiation
risk_factors.description: Minerals beneficiation (smelting, refining) requires cheap, reliable power. Eskom's tariff trajectory — driven by cost-recovery pricing — threatens the comparative advantage in energy-intensive industries.

quick_wins.title: FATF greylisting exit — sustained compliance
quick_wins.description: South Africa was removed from the FATF grey list in October 2024 following passage of the General Laws (Anti-Money Laundering and Combating Terrorism Financing) Amendment Act and the Financial Sector and Deposit Insurance Laws Amendment Act. Maintain compliance to prevent re-listing.
quick_wins.legislative_reference: General Laws Amendment Act 2022; Financial Sector Laws Amendment Act 2023

quick_wins.title: SARS capacity expansion — high-wealth and transfer pricing
quick_wins.description: Deploy 2,000 additional auditors targeting high-wealth individuals, transfer pricing, and VAT fraud. Operation Vulindlela and Zondo Commission identified SARS undercapacity as a major revenue leakage. Reversing the 2015–2018 capacity destruction.

quick_wins.title: SOE rationalization plan — Cabinet approval
quick_wins.description: Publish and secure Cabinet approval for a plan to reduce the national government SOE portfolio from 700+ entities to below 200, through mergers, liquidations, and privatisations. Addresses the contingent liability overhang.

quick_wins.title: PRASA turnaround — security and track rehabilitation
quick_wins.description: Implement the PRASA Recovery Plan Phase 2: security force deployment on Metrorail corridors, track rehabilitation on Johannesburg and Cape Town lines. PRASA ridership has collapsed from 2m to 200,000 daily trips since 2015.

quick_wins.title: Municipal technical support — 50 distressed municipalities
quick_wins.description: Deploy MISA technical support teams to 50 distressed municipalities covering engineering, finance, and supply chain management. Operation Vulindlela priority to restore basic service delivery.

quick_wins.title: Public Procurement Act 2023 — implementation regulations
quick_wins.description: Issue the secondary regulations required to operationalise the Public Procurement Act signed in 2023. Act consolidates Preferential Procurement Policy Framework and establishes the Office of the Chief Procurement Officer.

medium_term.title: SOE board governance — performance contracts and fit-and-proper
medium_term.description: Implement binding performance contracts for all SOE board members and executives, with public disclosure of targets and outcomes. Apply fit-and-proper standards using the Companies Act and King IV framework.

medium_term.title: SIU and NPA — consequence management for state capture
medium_term.description: Operationalise SIU civil recovery orders and NPA prosecutions identified in the Zondo Commission findings. Targets: R500bn in identified losses, estimated 1,000+ prosecutable matters. Requires NPA and SIU capacity investment.

medium_term.title: PRASA rail corridor restoration — full programme
medium_term.description: Restore Metrorail operations on Cape Town (all lines), Johannesburg (main corridors), and Tshwane lines by 2027. Requires R40bn capital programme for rolling stock, signalling, and station infrastructure.

medium_term.title: NPA capacity — commercial crime specialisation
medium_term.description: Increase NPA prosecutor complement by 500 and establish dedicated commercial crime prosecution units in all provinces. The NPA currently has a 40% vacancy rate in commercial crime units.

medium_term.title: Municipal finance — MFMA intervention protocols
medium_term.description: Standardise and accelerate COGTA's Section 139 interventions in financially distressed municipalities. Develop a graduated support framework from advisory to full administration.

medium_term.title: Government digital services — GovCloud implementation
medium_term.description: Migrate national government services to a shared cloud infrastructure, reducing IT fragmentation and enabling cross-departmental data integration. Precondition for e-government at scale.

long_term.title: Fiscal consolidation — debt-to-GDP below 75%
long_term.description: Stabilise gross national debt below 75% of GDP by FY2025/26 and put it on a declining trajectory. Requires sustained primary balance improvement, SOE bailout containment, and revenue growth from SARS compliance.

long_term.title: SOE portfolio privatisation — non-strategic entities
long_term.description: Complete sale or winding up of non-strategic SOEs (SA Forestry, SAA minority stake, Alexkor, Safcol) and partial privatisation of commercial SOEs. Reduces contingent liabilities by an estimated R200bn.

long_term.title: Anti-corruption institutions — statutory independence
long_term.description: Amend legislation to entrench the NPA's operational and budgetary independence and the DPCI (Hawks)'s independence from political interference. Implements Zondo Commission Recommendation 4.7.
long_term.legislative_reference: National Prosecuting Authority Act / SA Police Service Act

long_term.title: Subnational fiscal reform — equitable share formula revision
long_term.description: Revise the provincial and local government equitable share formula to better reflect service delivery costs, demographic change, and own-revenue capacity. Last major revision was 2009.

key_dependencies.to: Lower borrowing costs and correspondent banking
key_dependencies.description: Greylisting raised SA's risk premium by an estimated 25–50bps and impaired correspondent banking relationships. Exit (achieved October 2024) reduces fiscal pressure and improves banking access.
Every other reform package requires fiscal expenditure. SARS revenue performance is the single most important input to fiscal space — each 1% improvement in compliance yields ~R20bn.
Eskom's R254bn bailout and Transnet's distress absorb fiscal headroom that could fund infrastructure and human capital. SOE reform is a prerequisite for fiscal consolidation.

Without visible prosecutions and asset recovery from state capture, procurement reform norms lack credibility and rent-seeking continues. SIU recovery and NPA prosecutions are the enforcement mechanism.
Collapsed public rail forces workers into expensive and unreliable minibus taxi transport, limiting labour market access in township communities. Rail restoration is a spatial equity intervention.

success_metrics.metric: Gross national debt as % of GDP
Government tenders on Open Tender portal

risk_factors.title: Political will for SOE restructuring against union opposition
risk_factors.description: NUM, NUMSA, and COSATU have consistently opposed SOE privatisation and restructuring. Coalition government dynamics in the GNU could produce ministerial resistance to any SOE reform that threatens jobs.

risk_factors.title: New SOE bailout requests undermining fiscal consolidation
risk_factors.description: SAA, SABC, Denel, Transnet, and the Land Bank have recurring balance sheet crises. Each new bailout request erodes the fiscal consolidation pathway. NT's ability to say no depends on political cover from the Presidency.

risk_factors.title: Judicial and NPA capacity constraints
risk_factors.description: The NPA faces a 40% vacancy rate and persistent political interference allegations. Even with Zondo prosecutions prioritised, the judicial backlog means state capture consequences may take 8–10 years to materialise.

risk_factors.title: GNU coalition paralysis on structural reforms
risk_factors.description: The Government of National Unity coalition requires consensus across parties with divergent economic philosophies. Structural reforms (privatisation, NHI renegotiation, LRA amendment) face coalition veto risk.

risk_factors.title: PRASA governance and leadership continuity
risk_factors.description: PRASA has had repeated CEO and board changes that disrupted implementation. Recovery plan continuity requires stable governance for 5+ years — a historically difficult ask for SA parastatals.
